answer:
In the Smṛti corpus, *divya* (दिव्य; divine ordeal or trial by ordeal) refers to a superhuman (*adhaḥsthita*) form of proof invoked in legal disputes (*vyavahāra*) where human evidence—documents (*lekhya*), witnesses (*sākṣin*), possession (*bhoga*), or inference (*hetu*)—is unavailable, inconclusive, or inapplicable (e.g., secret crimes, thefts, violence, or doubts in testimony).[^1][^2][^3] It is subordinate to human proof but prescribed for grave accusations (*mahāpātaka*), hidden acts (*guhya*), or when equity demands divine intervention, often after oaths or reasoning fails.[^4] Ordeals test purity through elements (fire, water, etc.), with success (*utsṛjya*, exoneration) or failure (*doṣa*, guilt) determining the verdict. They are performed publicly before the king, assessors (*sabhā*), and Brahmins, with safeguards (fasting, purity rites). Common types across texts: balance (*tulā/dhaṭa*), fire (*agni*), water (*jala/udaka*), poison (*viṣa*), and variants like libation (*kośa*) or *dharma*-ordeal. Brāhmaṇas receive milder forms (e.g., no poison/fire). Failure incurs fines, corporal punishment, or death (non-Brāhmaṇa); success yields purity and costs from the accuser.[^5]

### Comparison Across Specified Smṛtis
| Smṛti          | Coverage of *Divya* | Key Types (5–9 listed) | Conditions for Use | Procedures/Notes |
|----------------|---------------------|-------------------------|---------------------|------------------|
| **Yājñavalkya** (Ch. 2, *divyaprakaraṇam*)[^6] | Detailed section (vv. 95–113); 5 core types. Emphasizes grave accusations (*mahāpātaka*), treason. Human proof preferred; *divya* when absent. | Balance (*tulā*: for weak/Brāhmaṇas); fire (*hutāṅgaraḥ*: iron ball walk); water (*jalam*: submersion); poison (*viṣam*: 7 grains); libation (*kośodakam*: deity-water, 14 days). | Stakes life; sunrise, bathed/fasted; experts administer. No *tulā* for debts <1000 *paṇa*s. | Balance: weigh, mark, re-weigh post-rites. Fire: 7 circles (16 *aṅgulas* each). Water: hold thighs in navel-deep. Purity if unburnt/submerged. |
| **Nārada** (Ch. 20; *divyāni*)[^7] | Extensive (vv. 1–48); 5 types (per Manu). For doubt (*saṃśaya*), secret (*guhya*), violence (*sāhasa*). Avoid if witnesses/docs available; daytime/public preferred. | Balance (*dhaṭa*); fire (*agni*); water (*udaka*); poison (*viṣa*: 7 grains *śārṅga*); consecrated water (*kośa*: deity-bath water, 14 days). | No witnesses/docs; plaintiff consent; fasted/pure. Season/person restrictions (no poison for weak). | Balance: 4x6-cubit frame; mark weight. Fire: 50-pala ball, 7x32-*aṅgula* circles. Water: arrow-fetch test. Poison: winter, ghee-mixed. *Kośa*: no calamity in 14 days. |
| **Bṛhaspati** (Ch. 1, §1.4)[^8] | Mentions 9 kinds (*navavidha*: balance to *dharma*-ordeal). Subordinate to human proof (witnesses/docs/inference). Use for doubts, secret/violent acts, docs/witness conflicts. | Lists "from balance (*tulā*) to *dharma*-ordeal" (unspecified; implies standard 5+). | No witnesses/docs; violence/theft/deposits; not verbal/land disputes. Ordeals clarify inference gaps. | Prefers human (*mānusam*); divine (*daivam*) when needed (e.g., perished witnesses). King examines even with witnesses in major sins. |
| **Viṣṇu** | None found. No *divya*/ordeal references in available text (e.g., Ch. 35 on sins). Corpus lacks procedural detail here.[^9] | N/A | N/A | N/A (focuses on sins, penances; no vyavahāra). |

**Key Similarities**: All prioritize human > divine proof; 5 core ordeals overlap (balance/fire/water/poison/libation-*kośa*). Public, ritualized; for secrecy/violence. Brāhmaṇa exemptions.

**Differences**: Y/N detailed rituals; Bṛhaspati expands to 9, stresses conditions; Viṣṇu silent (perhaps assumes Manu). Bṛhaspati most restrictive (avoid if docs/witnesses).

The corpus (37 works) covers *divya* variably; not exhaustive (e.g., no Katyāyana here, but similar). For unanswerable topics, corpus limits apply—no invention.[^10]

[^1]: Nārada Smṛti, Ch. 1, [p-983bae](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-983bae).

[^2]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1, [p-510297](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-510297).

[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 2, [p-0c3a85](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-0c3a85).

[^4]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1, [p-44a57a](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-44a57a).

[^5]: Nārada Smṛti, Ch. 1, [p-ef3be1](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-ef3be1).

[^6]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 2, [p-357a68](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-357a68) (full section to [p-1c5915](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-1c5915)).

[^7]: Nārada Smṛti, Ch. 1, [p-6ade8c](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-6ade8c) (full Ch. 20).

[^8]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1, [p-510297](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-510297)ff.

[^9]: Viṣṇu Smṛti searches/Ch. 35 yield no matches.

[^10]: General corpus note: 37 works; Manu (not queried) foundational (8 types).
